data.title: Canada Approves Gulfstream Jet Models Following U.S. President Trump's Remarks
data.description: Canada's aviation authority has granted certification for certain Gulfstream jet models. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows U.S. President Donald Trump's comments about Canada's previous refusal to approve the aircraft. Trump had warned of potential tariffs and other retaliatory actions if the situation was not resolved.

data.title: Ripple’s Garlinghouse Predicts 90% Likelihood CLARITY Act Will Pass by April, Boosting XRP Optimism
data.description:
Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse said he believes there is now a 90% likelihood that the Clarity Act will be approved before the end of April.
source: https://zycrypto.com/ripples-garlinghouse-predicts-90-likelihood-clarity-act-will-pass-by-april-boosting-xrp-optimism/

data.title: Dubai Real Estate Tokenization Goes Live on XRPL
data.description:
Ripple executive Reece Merrick confirmed on February 20, 2026, that the second phase of the Dubai Land Department’s (DLD) real estate tokenization initiative has officially launched on the XRP Ledger (XRPL). The expansion marks a significant step forward in bringing regulated property trading onto blockchain infrastructure.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/dubai-real-estate-tokenization-goes-live-on-xrpl/

data.title: OpenAI Forecasts Significant Revenue Growth by 2030
data.description: OpenAI is anticipating substantial revenue growth, with projections indicating it will surpass $280 billion by 2030, according to a source familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's optimistic outlook for the coming years. This forecast reflects OpenAI's strategic initiatives and expansion plans aimed at capitalizing on the increasing demand for artificial intelligence technologies. The company is focusing on enhancing its product offerings and expanding its market reach to achieve these ambitious financial targets. OpenAI's growth trajectory underscores the broader trend of rising interest and investment in AI-driven solutions across various industries.

data.title: Fed President Slams Crypto As ‘Utterly Useless’ Compared To AI, Labels Stablecoins A ‘Buzzword Salad’
data.description:
Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari on Thursday dismissed crypto as “utterly useless” in comparison to AI and tore into stablecoins.
source: https://zycrypto.com/fed-president-slams-crypto-as-utterly-useless-compared-to-ai-labels-stablecoins-a-buzzword-salad/

data.title: Claude Code Security Launches in Research Preview
data.description: Crypto Jargon posted on X. Claude Code Security has been launched in a research preview phase. The new tool offers AI-driven vulnerability scanning and provides patch suggestions to enhance code security. This development aims to assist developers in identifying and addressing potential security issues in their code efficiently. The tool is expected to streamline the process of securing software by leveraging artificial intelligence to detect vulnerabilities and recommend solutions.

data.title: 12 Malaysian Police Officers Arrested Over $51,000 Crypto Extortion Allegation
data.description:
Malaysian authorities have arrested 12 police officers over allegations that they extorted approximately 200,000 ringgit (about $51,000) in cryptocurrency from a group of Chinese nationals during a raid earlier this month. The arrests were officially announced on February 19, 2026, following an internal investigation into a late-night operation conducted in Kajang.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/12-malaysian-police-officers-arrested-over-51000-crypto-extortion-allegation/

data.title: Tariff refunds stay uncertain after IEEPA Supreme Court ruling
data.description:
After the 6-3 Supreme Court ruling curbing IEEPA tariffs, tariff refunds remain unresolved; outlines CBP claims steps, eligibility, timing, and market impacts.
source: https://coincu.com/news/tariff-refunds-stay-uncertain-after-ieepa-supreme-court-ruling/

data.title: Stanczak to Leave Ethereum Foundation; Bastian Aue Named Interim Co-Executive Director
data.description:
Tomasz Stanczak will leave the Ethereum Foundation at month’s end; Bastian Aue will serve as interim co-executive director as Ethereum advances AI, Layer 2 coordination, and institutional adoption.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/tomasz-stanczak-to-leave-ethereum-foundation-bastian-aue-named-interim-co-executive-director/

data.title: Netflix's Ted Sarandos Addresses Concerns Over Warner Bros. Acquisition
data.description: Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos has addressed concerns regarding the company's plans for movie releases if its acquisition of Warner Bros. proceeds. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Sarandos' response to apprehensions within Hollywood about the potential impact on theaters. Sarandos emphasized Netflix's commitment to maintaining a diverse range of distribution channels, including theatrical releases, to ensure that audiences have multiple ways to enjoy films. He reassured stakeholders that Netflix values the cinematic experience and intends to support it alongside its streaming services. The acquisition, if successful, could reshape the landscape of film distribution, with Netflix aiming to balance traditional and digital platforms.

data.title: Dollar Weakens as U.S. Supreme Court Rules Against Trump Tariffs
data.description: The U.S. dollar softened, ending a four-day streak of gains. According to RTHK, the U.S. Supreme Court has ruled against tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump. The dollar index was around 97.8, marking a 0.1% decline, though it rose approximately 1% over the week. The euro showed little change against the dollar on Friday, standing at $1.178. The British pound increased by about 0.2%, reaching $1.3482. Meanwhile, the dollar remained stable against the Japanese yen, quoted at 155.05 yen.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints Additional 250 Million USDC Stablecoins
data.description: The USDC Treasury has minted an additional 250 million USDC stablecoins, as reported by Whale Alert. According to NS3.AI, this substantial issuance suggests a rise in demand or liquidity requirements linked to USDC. Such expansions in stablecoin supply typically signal shifts in market activity or heightened institutional interest.

data.title: Trinseo Considers Debt Restructuring Amid Bankruptcy Possibility
data.description: Trinseo, a plastics manufacturer based in Pennsylvania, is reportedly in talks with creditors to restructure its debt. Bloomberg posted on X that the company is exploring options that may include filing for bankruptcy in the upcoming weeks, according to sources familiar with the matter. The discussions are part of Trinseo's efforts to address its financial challenges and ensure long-term stability. The potential restructuring aims to alleviate the company's debt burden and improve its financial health. Details of the negotiations and the exact timeline for any potential bankruptcy filing remain undisclosed. Trinseo's move comes amid broader industry pressures and economic uncertainties affecting the plastics sector.
